metadata.title: Why the Nondegenerate Ground State's Second-Order Shift Is Never Positive

skin_warnings: B15: palette=claude but the outro is 'un-annotated' — OUTRO LAW wants ClaudeTitleOutro

beats.label: This is Liam, in for Bear. Add an electric field to hydrogen's nondegenerate ground state. Symmetry

beats.prompt: The numerator is an absolute square. It can be positive, or exactly zero when the perturbation does not connect those two states. It can never be negative.
